children.heading: Wildlife law enforcement professional training and coordination activities
children.label: a Wildlife law enforcement professional training and coordination activities
children.text: The Secretary of State and the Administrator of the United States Agency for International Development, in collaboration with the heads of other relevant United States agencies and nongovernmental partners where appropriate, may provide assistance to focus countries to carry out the recommendations made in the strategic plan required by , among other goals, to improve the effectiveness of wildlife law enforcement in regions and countries that have demonstrated capacity, willingness, and need for assistance.section 7631(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Sense of Congress regarding security assistance to counter wildlife trafficking and poaching in Africa
children.label: b Sense of Congress regarding security assistance to counter wildlife trafficking and poaching in Africa
children.text: It is the sense of Congress that the United States should continue to provide defense articles (not including significant military equipment), defense services, and related training to appropriate security forces of countries of Africa for the purposes of countering wildlife trafficking and poaching.
